metadata.description: VORq Probabilistic Causal Knowledge Graph — 10 event types, 8 sectors

causal_links.mechanism: Primary fabs & R&D are concentrated in conflict zones — export controls freeze chip supply and IP licensing instantly.
Governments surge procurement; supply chains reorient as escalation unfolds.
Capital flight from Asia-Pacific, currency instability, sovereign credit watch triggered.
Taiwan Strait closure disrupts major global shipping lanes; freight rates spike.
Military spending surges across allied nations; defense contractors see immediate order backlogs.
Supply route disruptions and sanctions pressure global energy markets.
Risk-off sentiment drives capital flight; insurance and reinsurance costs spike.
Supply chain decoupling between two major markets disrupts component flows.
Both nations escalate defense procurement; allied nations increase military aid.
Indian Ocean and South China Sea shipping lanes face elevated risk.
European gas supplies severely disrupted; pipeline infrastructure at risk.
Black Sea grain corridor shut down; global wheat and fertilizer prices spike.
NATO nations dramatically increase defense spending and arms deliveries.
Trade bans restrict rare earth exports and block advanced chip designs from entering mainland markets.
Battery supply chains heavily reliant on Chinese processing face immediate margin pressure.
Trade volume contraction as tariffs reshape bilateral commerce patterns.
Russian oil embargo tightens global supply; spot prices surge on constrained output.
Fertilizer export restrictions cascade into higher food production costs globally.
SWIFT disconnections, asset freezes create counterparty risk across European banking.
Iranian crude removed from market tightens OPEC+ supply balance.
Shipping lanes and sanctions combine to disrupt global crude flows and refinery margins, spiking prices.
Spiking energy costs immediately translate to higher fertilizer prices and transport costs.
Bunker fuel costs surge; carriers impose emergency fuel surcharges across all routes.
High fuel prices accelerate EV adoption; but ICE-dependent manufacturers suffer.
Capital flight, currency volatility, sovereign downgrades hit bank exposure hard.
Vaccine and therapeutic demand surges; pharma revenues spike on emergency authorizations.
Port closures, labor shortages, and quarantine measures create massive backlogs.
Labor shortages at harvesting and processing plants; food distribution networks strained.
Crop destruction, farmland flooding, and harvest disruptions cause food price spikes.
Port damage, road/rail destruction halt supply chains for weeks in affected regions.
Insurance claims surge; rebuilding costs strain government budgets and credit ratings.
Component shortages cascade across electronics manufacturing; lead times extend to months.
Just-in-time manufacturing collapses as critical component inventories dry up.
Container shortages and port congestion amplify the disruption across all trade routes.
Political uncertainty triggers capital outflows, currency depreciation, and bond market selloffs.
Regime instability in energy-producing nations creates supply uncertainty.
Neighboring states increase military readiness and procurement in response to instability.
Bank runs, credit freezes, and systemic deleveraging trigger cascading failures.
Enterprise and consumer spending cuts reduce chip demand; capex freezes hit foundries.
Consumer credit tightening and falling demand slash vehicle sales across all segments.
IP theft, production halt, and trust erosion across digital infrastructure.
Banking system breaches freeze transactions; regulatory fines and remediation costs spike.
Ransomware attacks on port management and logistics systems cause days-long operational halt.
European gas supply crunch sends prices to record levels; LNG spot market surges.
Gas-based fertilizer production halts; food processing energy costs become unsustainable.
European factory output curtailed as gas rationing hits industrial consumers first.
Reduced tariffs and IP protections stimulate cross-border R&D and component trade.
Harmonized standards and lower duties open new markets for vehicle exports.
Removal of agricultural tariffs increases export volumes and stabilizes prices.
EV battery materials and micro-chip shortages cascade through production lines.
Fuel cost changes directly impact shipping margins and freight rates.
Credit tightening reduces capex available for fab construction and R&D.

Technology & Semiconductors: Accelerate nearshoring of critical fab infrastructure (CHIPS Act funding).
Stockpile legacy node chips for 18-month buffering.
Diversify rare earth mineral sourcing across multiple continents.
Automotive & EV: Redesign silicon architecture to rely on widely available analog chips.
Diversify battery mineral refining away from single-point bottlenecks.
Build strategic component reserves for 90-day production continuity.
Defense & Aerospace: Lock in long-term critical mineral contracts with allied nations.
Establish parallel supply chains in allied manufacturing hubs.
Energy & Oil: Hedge spot market exposure with 24-month futures.
Reroute strategic petroleum reserves to critical infrastructure.
Accelerate renewable energy capacity to reduce fossil fuel dependence.
Financial Services: Increase capital buffers and stress-test exposure to geopolitical shocks.
Diversify counterparty exposure across non-correlated markets.
Implement real-time sanctions screening and compliance automation.
Agriculture & Food: Establish strategic grain reserves at national level.
Diversify fertilizer suppliers across multiple producing nations.
Invest in precision agriculture to reduce input dependency.
Healthcare & Pharma: Build domestic API (active pharmaceutical ingredient) manufacturing capacity.
Maintain strategic medical supply stockpiles for 6-month surge capacity.
Diversify clinical trial sites across multiple geographies.
Logistics & Shipping: Develop alternative shipping corridor contingency plans.
Invest in nearshoring logistics to reduce long-haul dependency.
Deploy AI-based supply chain visibility platforms for real-time rerouting.
